title: Calonemorchis lobata
intro: Calonemorchis lobata, also known as the Lobed Calonema or Lobed Calonemorchis, is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Calonemorchis lobata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Calonemorchis lobata is commonly used in traditional herbal preparations, such as decoctions and infusions, to make teas and tonics that are valued for their therapeutic benefits.
taxonomy: Calonemorchis lobata is classified within the family Orchidaceae. This epiphytic orchid is a member of the subtribe Calonemorchidinae. Within the Orchidaceae family, Calonemorchis lobata is further classified into the genus Calonemorchis. The species Calonemorchis lobata is a part of the plant kingdom, specifically in the kingdom Plantae. The classification of Calonemorchis lobata reflects its unique characteristics and relationships within the plant kingdom.
